topic.name: Student Financial Aid Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at reforming and enhancing student financial aid and loan processes in higher education.

Document 1: Student Loan Relief Act of 2015 authorizes refinancing of federal student loans into private market.
Treasury to begin a national awareness campaign about refinancing program.
Document 2: Understanding the True Cost of College Act requires standardized financial aid offer forms for institutions.
Form must include details on cost of attendance, types of loans, and repayment obligations.
Document 3: Reducing Barriers for Veterans Education Act provides assistance with application fees for veterans.
Document 4: Empowering Students Through Enhanced Financial Counseling Act modifies loan counseling requirements for institutions.
Document 5: Help Student Soldiers Act waives loan return requirements for students withdrawing due to military service.
Document 6: Know Before You Owe Private Student Loan Act mandates lender certification of student enrollment and costs.
Document 7: DELIVER Act directs VA to assist with loan payments for employees during service.
Document 8: Bank on Students Emergency Loan Refinancing Act establishes refinancing program for federal student loans.
Document 9: Understanding the True Cost of College Act of 2012 requires detailed disclosures on financial aid offers.
Document 10: SUCCESS Act repeals ineligibility for student aid due to drug offenses.

topic.name: Labor and Voting Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to employee rights in labor relations and voting processes in federal elections.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to certify a bargaining representative without directing an election if a majority of the bargaining unit employees have authorized designation of the representative.
Revises enforcement requirements with respect to unfair labor practices during union organizing drives.
Document 2: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to require state and local election officials to consult with the U.S. Postal Service in designing absentee ballots.
Requires procedures to track and confirm receipt of absentee ballots.
Document 3: Prohibits state or local election officials from preventing an individual from registering or voting based solely on voter caging documents.
Defines 'voter caging document' and outlines requirements for challenges to an individual's registration.
Document 4: Establishes the first Saturday and Sunday after the first Friday in November as election days for Congress and presidential elections.
Amends federal laws to establish the same polling place hours for elections.
Document 5: Makes it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation.
Adds a requirement that representatives be selected by secret ballot in an election conducted by the NLRB.
Document 6: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation.
Defines 'majority' for purposes of determining the majority of employees in an election.
Document 7: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 to require civil actions brought by beneficiaries to be adjudicated as de novo proceedings.
Document 8: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act to make it unlawful for a chief state election administration official to take active part in political management.
Document 9: Requires a final decision of the VA regarding adverse personnel action against a VA employee to be issued within 60 days of appeal.
Subjects such decisions to judicial review.
Document 10: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation.
Requires the NLRB to give 14 days advance notice before a hearing when investigating an election petition.

topic.name: Electric Vehicle and Emissions Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation and programs aimed at promoting electric vehicles, reducing greenhouse gas emissions, and enhancing alternative fuel infrastructure.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Energy to establish a program to deploy and integrate plug-in electric drive vehicles
to facilitate the integration of advanced vehicle technologies into electricity distribution areas
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to impose a $10 per ton excise tax on the extraction of coal
allows expenditures from the Coal Mitigation Trust Fund to assist states in mitigating pollution and other threats to public health
Document 3: Requires U.S. coal producers, oil refinery operators, and others to purchase a federal emission permit for GHG emission substances
Establishes emission reduction targets for 2016 through 2060 decreasing from 90% to 20%
Document 4: Establishes within the Department of Energy a national plug-in electric drive vehicle deployment program
provides technical assistance upon request to state, local, and tribal governments with the deployment of plug-in electric drive vehicles
Document 5: Requires automobile manufacturers to ensure that at least 50% of 2015 and 2016 model year automobiles are dual fueled
Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program
Document 6: Requires the Administrator of the EPA to auction 10% of emission allowances to implement state greenhouse gas emission reduction plans
Directs states to establish goals for reducing greenhouse gas emissions from the transportation sector
Document 7: Amends the Energy Independence and Security Act to repeal the requirement that federal agency procurement contracts specify lifecycle GHG emissions
Authorizes the Secretary of Defense to enter into contracts for alternative fuels
Document 8: Establishes a Building Pollution Reduction Program to assist building owners in reducing emissions
Requires the Administrator to provide assistance based on verifiable reductions in emissions
Document 9: Creates incentives for the manufacture of dual-fueled automobiles that use alternative fuels
Removes the cap on credits under the Corporate Average Fuel Economy program for non-alcohol, dual-fueled automobiles
Document 10: Revises the renewable fuel program and requires the EPA to promulgate regulations for advanced biofuels
Requires each light-duty automobile manufacturer's annual covered inventory to be comprised of at least 30% fuel choice-enabling automobiles by 2013

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving and regulating Medicare and Medicaid services, including access, coverage, and quality of care.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to accept the results of a state licensure survey for purposes of determining federal certification of the compliance of a renal dialysis facility.
Revises Medicare requirements for group health plans to extend from 12 to 42 months after an individual becomes eligible for Medicare part A.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish a Federal Coordinated Health Care Office within the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services.
Amends Part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of SSA title XVIII to direct the HHS Secretary to offer one or more Medicare operated prescription drug plans.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to request the National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) to conduct reviews every five years of the national and state-specific markets for long-term care (LTC) insurance policies.

Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to require a state Medicaid plan to provide for the annual submission of data relating to functional criteria for the receipt of nursing facility services under the plan.
Document 4: Amends part C (Medicare+Choice) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to extend through December 31, 2013, the authority to restrict enrollment for specialized Medicare Advantage plans.
Requires the Secretary to provide bonus payments to account for added SNP costs associated with additional benefit, care management, reporting, and other requirements.
Document 5: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to add a new part E (Purchase of Medicare Benefits by Certain Individuals 55 to 65 Years of Age).
Creates in the Treasury the Medicare Early Access Trust Fund to hold the premiums collected under this Act and to support the new program.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services, with respect to air ambulance services, to reduce the mandatory percentage increase for payments under the fee schedule by 2.0% for any supplier or provider that fails to submit to the Secretary specified data.
The Secretary must also adjust such percentages to ensure that the increased expenditures under this Act are equal to the reduced expenditures.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Service to conduct a demonstration program requiring a state to establish a process for joint training and education of surveyors and providers for skilled nursing facilities.
Amends title XVIII (Medicare) and title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to allow resumption of nursing aid training programs in SNFs after correction of deficiencies.
Document 8: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to prescribe a formula for determining the payment amount for a multiple source or a single source sterile injectable drug product.
Directs the Secretary to contract with an independent entity to study the effects of this Act on patient access to sterile products.
Document 9: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to direct each state to implement an asset verification program requiring Medicaid applicants or recipients to authorize the state to obtain records from a financial institution.
Requires states to submit to the Secretary state plan amendments for creating Internet-based transparency programs to improve public disclosure of Medicaid payments information.
Document 10: Revises and extends through FY2019 the program under title XXI (State Children's Health Insurance) (CHIP) of the Social Security Act.
Directs the Secretary to issue regulations to ensure continuity of care for children who are undergoing an active course of treatment.

topic.name: Drug Trafficking and Crime Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislation aimed at combating drug trafficking, enhancing penalties for related crimes, and addressing issues of violent crime and gang activity.

Document 1: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to increase prison terms for manufacturing, distributing, or possessing large amounts of drugs.
Directs the Attorney General to develop a National Drug Trafficking Enforcement Strategy.
Document 2: Increase criminal penalties for transferring firearms for use in crimes of violence or drug trafficking.
Authorize the Attorney General to impose civil fines on firearms licensees who willfully violate federal firearms laws.
Document 4: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to increase prison terms for drug trafficking offenses.
Directs the Attorney General to develop a National Drug Trafficking Enforcement Strategy.
Document 6: Impose or increase criminal penalties for committing violent crimes in aid of a criminal street gang or racketeering activity.
Directs the Attorney General to expand the Project Safe Neighborhoods program to prosecute significant criminal street gangs.

topic.description: The documents collectively address various legislative measures and policies aimed at imposing sanctions on Iran due to its nuclear activities, support for terrorism, and human rights abuses.

Document 1: the United States should continue to support diplomatic efforts in the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) and the U.N. Security Council to end Iran's illicit nuclear activities
the President is urged to impose sanctions on the Central Bank of Iran
Document 2: This bill prohibits the Department of the Treasury from authorizing U.S. financial institution transactions in connection with the export or re-export of commercial passenger aircraft to the Islamic Republic of Iran

the Export-Import Bank from guaranteeing, insuring, extending credit, or participating in the extension of credit in connection with the export of U.S. goods or services sought by the Government of Iran
Document 5: The President shall impose against Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) sanctions with respect to: blocking property of, and prohibiting transactions with, foreign persons who commit or support terrorism

The President shall impose specified sanctions against a person identified by the Department of State in an annual report as having acted to undermine, or as posing a risk of undermining: the peace or stability in Bahrain, Iraq, Syria, Jordan, Kuwait, Lebanon, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates, or Yemen
Document 6: expands the list of persons subject to sanctions for human rights abuses and other acts of violence
subjects listed individuals to the blocking of property and property interests that are in the United States
Document 7: expresses the sense of Congress concerning a variety of existing and proposed sanctions against Iran
Subjects Iran to specified economic sanctions unless the President certifies to Congress that Iran has verifiably dismantled all nuclear enrichment-related and reprocessing-related programs
Document 8: This bill prohibits the President from paying final judgments, awards, or compromise settlements to Iran
The President must certify before providing or authorizing payment of any funds to Iran that: the funds will not be used to support international terrorism
Document 9: advocacy within the governing bodies of international organizations and other foreign policy settings for the full compensation and fair treatment of persons in whose favor judgments have been awarded by U.S. courts
protection of economic interests of persons and nations that benefit from a reliable flow of foreign capital by restricting the access to U.S. capital markets of judgment evading foreign states

topic.name: Air Transportation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative measures aimed at improving safety, security, and operational efficiency within the air transportation system in the United States.

Document 1: Minimum Staffing of Air Traffic Controllers Act of 2015 directs the Department of Transportation to ensure a minimum number of air traffic controllers at airports.
Document 2: Arm All Pilots Act of 2015 revises federal flight deck officer program requirements and training.
DHS shall designate additional training facilities for firearms training for federal flight deck officers.
Document 3: NextGen Flight Path Review and Notification Act of 2016 requires FAA to consult with airport operators and communities regarding new flight path procedures.
Document 4: PLANES Act of 2010 directs DHS to study and report on explosives detection technologies for screening air passengers and cargo.
Document 5: Air Travelers' Bill of Rights Act of 2012 establishes rights for air passengers during screening processes.
Document 6: Restoring Local Control of Airports Act of 2016 revises the Airport Improvement Program and local user fees.
Document 9: Free Market Flights Act of 2016 terminates the Department of Transportation's essential air service program.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to agricultural policy, including commodity programs, livestock reporting, and reforestation efforts.

Document 1: Repeals, but continues for crop year 2013: (1) direct payments, (2) countercyclical payments, and (3) the average crop revenue election program (ACRE).
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to provide for a farm's base acreage adjustment for covered commodities.
Document 2: Authorizes the President to provide the government of Haiti with financial assistance, technology transfers, or capacity building assistance to reduce deforestation and increase afforestation and reforestation.
Requires the President, in cooperation with the government of Haiti, to develop proposal performance metrics.
Document 3: Requires the Department of Agriculture (USDA) to establish an urban agriculture outreach program to award grants to support outreach activities.
USDA must give preference to grants for activities in historically underserved communities.
Document 4: Defines specified terms related to commodity programs.
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (Secretary) to adjust base acres for covered commodities for a farm.

Document 8: exclude from gross income 2% of the compensation of members of the Armed Forces.
Document 9: tax deduction for reimbursable expenses and court costs in contingency fee cases.
Document 10: work opportunity tax credit for hiring a member of the Ready Reserve or the National Guard.

topic.name: Maritime and Environmental Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation related to maritime operations, environmental protection, and fisheries management.

Document 1: Amends the Longshore and Harbor Workers' Compensation Act to define 'recreational vessel'
Requires the Secretary of Labor to revise a certain federal regulation
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2012-FY2023 to provide for the annual base funding for the intergovernmental recovery implementation program
for the endangered fish species in the Upper Colorado River
Document 3: Makes permanent a temporary exemption from permit requirements of the National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System
Exemption applies to a vessel that is less than 79 feet in length
Document 4: Prohibits the Department of Commerce from requiring sectors of the Northeast Multispecies Fishery to comply with the independent, third-party monitoring program
monitors annual catch limits for fishing vessels
Document 5: Revises certain minimum pipeline safety standards requirements

Document 6: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to increase the percentage of the aggregate amount made available for state drinking water treatment revolving loan funds

Document 7: Exempts Indian tribes from any requirement to provide compensatory mitigation as a condition of a permit

Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to issue a loan to refinance the existing debt obligation funding the fishing capacity reduction program

Document 10: Amends the Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act to provide that no permit shall be required for the use of a pesticide that is registered

topic.name: Retirement and Social Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing retirement benefits, Social Security provisions, and support for older workers and unemployed individuals.

Document 1: Older Worker Opportunity Act of 2007 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow employers who maintain a tax-qualified pension or retirement plan and who provide health insurance coverage a business-related tax credit for 25% of the first $6,000 of the wages of employees who have attained the age of 62.
Defines 'formal flexible work program' as a work program that consists of core and flex time.
Document 2: S.O.S. Act of 2016 - This bill amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) of the Social Security Act to increase the contribution and benefit base for 2017 through 2019.
The retirement age shall increase past 67 years by certain formulae.
Document 3: Assistance for Unemployed Workers Extension Act - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to extend the Emergency Unemployment Compensation (EUC) program through December 31, 2010.
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend federal-state agreements increasing regular unemployment compensation payments.
Document 4: Family and Retirement Health Investment Act of 2008 - Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to health savings accounts (HSAs) to permit holders of HSAs and their spouses who are age 55 or older to make an additional contribution.
Allow Medicare beneficiaries to continue to contribute to HSA accounts after reaching age 65.
Document 5: Family and Retirement Health Investment Act of 2011 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to health savings accounts (HSAs), to allow both spouses to make increased catch-up contributions to the same HSA.
Payments from an HSA for prescription and over-the-counter medicine or drugs.
Document 6: SWEPT Act of 2016 - This bill amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) of the Social Security Act with respect to the exception to the windfall elimination provision.
Revises the formula for computation of an individual's primary insurance benefit amount.
Document 7: Retirement and Income Security Enhancements (RAISE) Act - Amends title II of the Social Security Act to entitle divorced spouses to OASDI benefits after 5 years of marriage.
Revises the widow's or widower's insurance benefit for any month to equal the greater of the primary insurance amount of a deceased individual.
Document 8: Strengthening Social Security Act of 2013 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to prescribe special rules for the determination of taxable wages and self-employment income above the contribution and benefit base after 2013.
Directs the Bureau of Labor Statistics to prepare and publish the Consumer Price Index for Elderly Consumers.
Document 9: Family Fairness Act of 2008 - Amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 to make an employee eligible for coverage under such Act if that employee has been employed for at least 12 months.
Eliminates the requirement that the employee have served at least 1,250 hours during the 12-month period before the leave request.
Document 10: Responsible Unemployment Extension Act - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account.
Extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation program.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments

Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce from 7.5 to 2.5% the amount of adjusted gross income over which a taxpayer's medical expenses are fully deductible.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the tax deduction for the start-up expenditures of an active trade or business from $5,000 to $20,000
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow taxpayers who are part-time students to claim the tax credit for dependent care expenses.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individual taxpayers who are age 55 or older an income-based tax credit for qualified tuition and related expenses.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit for 50% of any contribution to a tax-exempt scholarship funding trust.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the child tax credit, to adjust the earned income threshold.
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income payments received by a student under a comprehensive student work-learning-service program.
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individual taxpayers a new refundable tax credit based on the number of their qualifying children under the age of three.
Document 10: Allows the increased tax deduction amount for business start-up expenses for taxable years beginning in 2015 or 2016.

Document 7: Extends federal recognition to the Chinook Indian Nation

Document 9: Amends the Gila Bend Indian Reservation Lands Replacement Act

Document 10: Vests title to real property acquired under the Alaska Kiln Drying Grant Program in the grant recipient.

Document 5: Directs the President, acting through the Secretary of State, to grant a permit under Executive Order 13337 for the Keystone XL pipeline project

topic.name: Tax Incentives for Energy and Vehicles
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various tax incentives and regulations related to energy efficiency, alternative fuels, and vehicle technologies.

Document 6: allow a tax credit for 10% of the cost of a qualified plug-in hybrid vehicle

Document 7: eliminate after 2010 the reduction in the rate of the tax credit for electricity produced from renewable resources

Document 8: allow a general business tax credit for 30% of the basis of an eligible lightweight coal freight car

Document 9: repeal the limitation on the number of new qualified hybrid and advanced lean-burn technology vehicles

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various consumer protection measures related to telecommunications, trade, and technology.

Document 1: Amends the African Growth and Opportunity Act (AGOA) to add Mauritius as a lesser developed beneficiary sub-Saharan African country (LDC) for purposes of the application of preferential treatment to apparel articles...

Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require commercial mobile service providers to make available on smartphones...

Prohibits a smartphone from being manufactured in the United States or imported into the United States for sale or resale to the public, unless the smartphone is configured in such a manner that a service provider may comply with such functionality requirements.

Document 6: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to direct the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to promulgate regulations requiring Internet service providers (ISPs) to provide plain language disclosures to assist consumers in making informed decisions about the purchase of Internet service...

Prohibits ISPs, multichannel video programming distributors (MVPDs), online video distributors (OVDs), and video programming vendors (VPVs), in various circumstances and in their relationships with one another, from engaging in unfair methods of competition, or unfair or deceptive acts or practices...
Document 7: Repeals a Library of Congress (LOC) rulemaking determination... regarding the circumvention of technological measures controlling access to copyrighted software on wireless telephone handsets...

Document 10: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to set the policy of the United States regarding various aspects of the Internet, including access, consumer choice, competition...
Makes it the duty of each Internet access service provider to... not block, interfere with, discriminate against, impair, or degrade the ability of any person to use an Internet access service.

topic.name: Regulatory Reform Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at reforming federal regulatory processes to reduce burdens on businesses and improve transparency.

Document 10: Amends the Regulatory Flexibility Act to revise the definition of 'rule' under such Act

topic.name: Pharmaceutical Regulation and Access
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving the regulation of pharmaceuticals, enhancing access to medications, and ensuring public health safety.

Document 1: amends the Public Health Service Act to permit the Department of Health and Human Services to make grants to states that allow standing orders for opioid overdose reversal medication

topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures aimed at improving safety and health standards in mining and related industries.

Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Labor to promulgate an interim final standard regulating combustible dusts, which shall apply to manufacturing, processing, blending, conveying, repackaging, and handling of combustible particulate solids.

Document 8: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 to require the Secretary to determine why an accident occurred and whether there were violations of law, mandatory health and safety standards, or other requirements.
